34: //TODO:
35: // Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate
36: // on collections and those that operate on individually named items.
37: // Collection interfaces:
38: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)
39: //    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter
40: //    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher
41: //    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater
42: //    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter
43: //    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater
44: //
45: // Single item interfaces:
46: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)
47: //    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter
48: //    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher
49: //    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater
50: //    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter
51: //    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater
52: 
53: // Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.
54: // Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected
55: // that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.
56: //
57: // Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.
58: type Storage interface {
59: 	// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.
60: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
61: 	New() runtime.Object
62: 
63: 	// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.
64: 	// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared
65: 	// for being called more than once.
66: 	Destroy()
67: }
68: 
69: // StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.
70: type StorageWithReadiness interface {
71: 	Storage
72: 
73: 	// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.
74: 	ReadinessCheck() error
75: }
76: 
77: // Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.
78: // It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.
79: type Scoper interface {
80: 	// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced
81: 	NamespaceScoped() bool
82: }
83: 
84: // KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external
85: // objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for
86: // the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of
87: // API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.
88: type KindProvider interface {
89: 	Kind() string
90: }
91: 
92: // ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.
93: type ShortNamesProvider interface {
94: 	ShortNames() []string
95: }
96: 
97: // CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can
98: // be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. "all" could translate to "pod,rc,svc,...").
99: type CategoriesProvider interface {
100: 	Categories() []string
101: }
102: 
103: // SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular
104: // name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.
105: type SingularNameProvider interface {
106: 	GetSingularName() string
107: }
108: 
109: // GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints
110: // which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.
111: // This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.
112: // TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.
113: type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {
114: 	GroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind
115: }
116: 
117: // GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.
118: // This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).
119: // The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.
120: type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {
121: 	AcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool
122: }
123: 
124: // Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.
125: type Lister interface {
126: 	// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.
127: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
128: 	NewList() runtime.Object
129: 	// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.
130: 	List(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
131: 	// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion
132: 	TableConvertor
133: }
134: 
135: // Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.
136: type Getter interface {
137: 	// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.
138: 	// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the
139: 	// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.
140: 	Get(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
141: }
142: 
143: // GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes
144: // additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the
145: // subpath of the GET request.
146: type GetterWithOptions interface {
147: 	// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.
148: 	// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the
149: 	// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.
150: 	// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions

59: // FinishFunc is a function returned by Begin hooks to complete an operation.
60: type FinishFunc func(ctx context.Context, success bool)
61: 
62: // AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.
63: type AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)
64: 
65: // BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.
66: type BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)
67: 
68: // AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.
69: type AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)
70: 
71: // BeginUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginUpdate hook.
72: type BeginUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)
73: 
74: // AfterUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterUpdate hook.
75: type AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)

77: // GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.
78: type GenericStore interface {
79: 	GetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy
80: 	GetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy
81: 	GetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy
82: }
83: 
84: // Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's
85: // intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any
86: // non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be
87: // copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same
88: // underlying behavior.
89: //
90: // All fields are required unless specified.
91: //
92: // The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific
93: // RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike
94: // resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and
95: // semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and
96: // RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic
97: // specific to the API.
98: //
99: // TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage
100: type Store struct {
101: 	// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a
102: 	// GET of a single object, e.g.:
103: 	//
104: 	// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object
105: 	NewFunc func() runtime.Object
106: 
107: 	// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the
108: 	// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:
109: 	//
110: 	// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource
111: 	NewListFunc func() runtime.Object
112: 
113: 	// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.
114: 	// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.
115: 	// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.
116: 	DefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource
117: 
118: 	// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.
119: 	SingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource
120: 
121: 	// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not
122: 	// include trailing "/".  This is used for operations that work on the
123: 	// entire collection (listing and watching).
124: 	//
125: 	// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.
126: 	KeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string
127: 
128: 	// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.
129: 	// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'
130: 	// can be gotten from ctx.
131: 	//
132: 	// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.
133: 	KeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)
134: 
135: 	// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.
136: 	ObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)
137: 
138: 	// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted
139: 	// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this
140: 	// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation
141: 	// against an existing object.
142: 	//
143: 	// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.
144: 	TTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)
145: 
146: 	// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels
147: 	// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the
148: 	// object matches the given field and label selectors.
149: 	PredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate
150:

17: package storage

25: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"

34: // Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.
35: type Feature = string
36: 
37: // RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.
38: var RequestWatchProgress Feature = "RequestWatchProgress"
39: 
40: // Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response
41: // onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an
42: // object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be
43: // a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,
44: // intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of
45: // type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.
46: type Versioner interface {
47: 	// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object
48: 	// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata
49: 	// from database.
50: 	UpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error
51: 	// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object
52: 	// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from
53: 	// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client
54: 	// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number
55: 	// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during
56: 	// serialization if it is set to nil.
57: 	UpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error
58: 	// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should
59: 	// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.
60: 	PrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error
61: 	// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.

151: 	// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete

266: // NamespaceKeyRootFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths
267: // to resource directories enforcing namespace rules.
268: func NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string) string {
269: 	key := prefix
270: 	ns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)
271: 	if ok && len(ns) > 0 {
272: 		key = key + "/" + ns
273: 	}
274: 	return key
275: }
276: 
277: // NamespaceKeyFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths to
278: // a resource relative to the given prefix enforcing namespace rules. If the
279: // context does not contain a namespace, it errors.
280: func NamespaceKeyFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string, name string) (string, error) {
281: 	key := NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx, prefix)
282: 	ns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)
283: 	if !ok || len(ns) == 0 {
284: 		return "", apierrors.NewBadRequest("Namespace parameter required.")
285: 	}
286: 	if len(name) == 0 {
287: 		return "", apierrors.NewBadRequest("Name parameter required.")
288: 	}
289: 	if msgs := content.IsPathSegmentName(name); len(msgs) != 0 {
290: 		return "", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf("Name parameter invalid: %q: %s", name, strings.Join(msgs, ";")))
291: 	}
292: 	key = key + "/" + name
293: 	return key, nil
294: }

17: package server
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"sync"
21: )
22: 
23: /*
24: We make an attempt here to identify the events that take place during
25: lifecycle of the apiserver.
26: 
27: We also identify each event with a name so we can refer to it.
28: 
29: Events:
30: - ShutdownInitiated: KILL signal received
31: - AfterShutdownDelayDuration: shutdown delay duration has passed
32: - InFlightRequestsDrained: all in flight request(s) have been drained
33: - HasBeenReady is signaled when the readyz endpoint succeeds for the first time
34: 
35: The following is a sequence of shutdown events that we expect to see with
36:   'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' = false:
37: 
38: T0: ShutdownInitiated: KILL signal received
39: 	- /readyz starts returning red
40:     - run pre shutdown hooks
41: 
42: T0+70s: AfterShutdownDelayDuration: shutdown delay duration has passed
43: 	- the default value of 'ShutdownDelayDuration' is '70s'
44: 	- it's time to initiate shutdown of the HTTP Server, server.Shutdown is invoked
45: 	- as a consequene, the Close function has is called for all listeners
46:  	- the HTTP Server stops listening immediately
47: 	- any new request arriving on a new TCP socket is denied with
48:       a network error similar to 'connection refused'
49:     - the HTTP Server waits gracefully for existing requests to complete
50:       up to '60s' (dictated by ShutdownTimeout)
51: 	- active long running requests will receive a GOAWAY.
52: 
53: T0+70s: HTTPServerStoppedListening:
54: 	- this event is signaled when the HTTP Server has stopped listening
55:       which is immediately after server.Shutdown has been invoked
56: 
57: T0 + 70s + up-to 60s: InFlightRequestsDrained: existing in flight requests have been drained
58: 	- long running requests are outside of this scope
59: 	- up-to 60s: the default value of 'ShutdownTimeout' is 60s, this means that
60:       any request in flight has a hard timeout of 60s.
61: 	- it's time to call 'Shutdown' on the audit events since all
62: 	  in flight request(s) have drained.
63: 
64: 
65: The following is a sequence of shutdown events that we expect to see with
66:   'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' = true:
67: 
68: T0: ShutdownInitiated: KILL signal received
69: 	- /readyz starts returning red
70:     - run pre shutdown hooks
71: 
72: T0+70s: AfterShutdownDelayDuration: shutdown delay duration has passed
73: 	- the default value of 'ShutdownDelayDuration' is '70s'
74: 	- the HTTP Server will continue to listen
75: 	- the apiserver is not accepting new request(s)
76: 		- it includes new request(s) on a new or an existing TCP connection
77: 		- new request(s) arriving after this point are replied with a 429
78:       	  and the  response headers: 'Retry-After: 1` and 'Connection: close'
79: 	- note: these new request(s) will not show up in audit logs
80: 
81: T0 + 70s + up to 60s: InFlightRequestsDrained: existing in flight requests have been drained
82: 	- long running requests are outside of this scope
83: 	- up to 60s: the default value of 'ShutdownTimeout' is 60s, this means that
84:       any request in flight has a hard timeout of 60s.
85: 	- server.Shutdown is called, the HTTP Server stops listening immediately
86:     - the HTTP Server waits gracefully for existing requests to complete
87:       up to '2s' (it's hard coded right now)
88: */
89: 
90: // lifecycleSignal encapsulates a named apiserver event
91: type lifecycleSignal interface {
92: 	// Signal signals the event, indicating that the event has occurred.
93: 	// Signal is idempotent, once signaled the event stays signaled and
94: 	// it immediately unblocks any goroutine waiting for this event.
95: 	Signal()
96: 
97: 	// Signaled returns a channel that is closed when the underlying event
98: 	// has been signaled. Successive calls to Signaled return the same value.
99: 	Signaled() <-chan struct{}
100: 
101: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@b08fa0cdf18610be74d9741cf361446a5f42ff76/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/lifecycle_signals.go%3FL1-100"}

{"content":"100: 
101: 	// Name returns the name of the signal, useful for logging.
102: 	Name() string
103: }
104: 
105: // lifecycleSignals provides an abstraction of the events that
106: // transpire during the lifecycle of the apiserver. This abstraction makes it easy
107: // for us to write unit tests that can verify expected graceful termination behavior.
108: //
109: // GenericAPIServer can use these to either:
110: //   - signal that a particular termination event has transpired
111: //   - wait for a designated termination event to transpire and do some action.
112: type lifecycleSignals struct {
113: 	// ShutdownInitiated event is signaled when an apiserver shutdown has been initiated.
114: 	// It is signaled when the `stopCh` provided by the main goroutine
115: 	// receives a KILL signal and is closed as a consequence.
116: 	ShutdownInitiated lifecycleSignal
117: 
118: 	// AfterShutdownDelayDuration event is signaled as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration
119: 	// has elapsed since the ShutdownInitiated event.
120: 	// ShutdownDelayDuration allows the apiserver to delay shutdown for some time.
121: 	AfterShutdownDelayDuration lifecycleSignal
122: 
123: 	// PreShutdownHooksStopped event is signaled when all registered
124: 	// preshutdown hook(s) have finished running.
125: 	PreShutdownHooksStopped lifecycleSignal
126: 
127: 	// NotAcceptingNewRequest event is signaled when the server is no
128: 	// longer accepting any new request, from this point on any new
129: 	// request will receive an error.
130: 	NotAcceptingNewRequest lifecycleSignal
131: 
132: 	// InFlightRequestsDrained event is signaled when the existing requests
133: 	// in flight have completed. This is used as signal to shut down the audit backends
134: 	InFlightRequestsDrained lifecycleSignal
135: 
136: 	// HTTPServerStoppedListening termination event is signaled when the
137: 	// HTTP Server has stopped listening to the underlying socket.
138: 	HTTPServerStoppedListening lifecycleSignal
139: 
140: 	// HasBeenReady is signaled when the readyz endpoint succeeds for the first time.
141: 	HasBeenReady lifecycleSignal
142: 
143: 	// MuxAndDiscoveryComplete is signaled when all known HTTP paths have been installed.
144: 	// It exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.
145: 	// The actual logic is implemented by an APIServer using the generic server library.
146: 	MuxAndDiscoveryComplete lifecycleSignal
147: }
148: 
149: // ShuttingDown returns the lifecycle signal that is signaled when
150: // the server is not accepting any new requests.
151: // this is the lifecycle event that is exported to the request handler
152: // logic to indicate that the server is shutting down.
153: func (s lifecycleSignals) ShuttingDown() <-chan struct{} {
154: 	return s.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled()
155: }
156: 
157: // newLifecycleSignals returns an instance of lifecycleSignals interface to be used
158: // to coordinate lifecycle of the apiserver
159: func newLifecycleSignals() lifecycleSignals {
160: 	return lifecycleSignals{
161: 		ShutdownInitiated:          newNamedChannelWrapper("ShutdownInitiated"),
162: 		AfterShutdownDelayDuration: newNamedChannelWrapper("AfterShutdownDelayDuration"),
163: 		PreShutdownHooksStopped:    newNamedChannelWrapper("PreShutdownHooksStopped"),
164: 		NotAcceptingNewRequest:     newNamedChannelWrapper("NotAcceptingNewRequest"),
165: 		InFlightRequestsDrained:    newNamedChannelWrapper("InFlightRequestsDrained"),
166: 		HTTPServerStoppedListening: newNamedChannelWrapper("HTTPServerStoppedListening"),
167: 		HasBeenReady:               newNamedChannelWrapper("HasBeenReady"),
168: 		MuxAndDiscoveryComplete:    newNamedChannelWrapper("MuxAndDiscoveryComplete"),
169: 	}
170: }
171: 
172: func newNamedChannelWrapper(name string) lifecycleSignal {
173: 	return &namedChannelWrapper{
174: 		name: name,
175: 		once: sync.Once{},
176: 		ch:   make(chan struct{}),
177: 	}
178: }
179: 
180: type namedChannelWrapper struct {
181: 	name string
182: 	once sync.Once
183: 	ch   chan struct{}
184: }
185: 
186: func (e *namedChannelWrapper) Signal() {
187: 	e.once.Do(func() {
188: 		close(e.ch)
189: 	})
190: }
191: 
192: func (e *namedChannelWrapper) Signaled() <-chan struct{} {
193: 	return e.ch
194: }
195: 
196: func (e *namedChannelWrapper) Name() string {
197: 	return e.name
198: }

68: // Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).
69: type action struct {
70: 	Verb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).
71: 	Path          string               // The path of the action
72: 	Params        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.

77: func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {
78: 	var apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery
79: 	parentResources := make(map[string]int)
80: 
81: 	// Loop through all top-level resources
82: 	for _, r := range list {
83: 		if strings.Contains(r.Name, "/") {
84: 			// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources
85: 			continue
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		var scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope
89: 		if r.Namespaced {
90: 			scope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace
91: 		} else {
92: 			scope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster
93: 		}
94: 
95: 		resource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{
96: 			Resource: r.Name,
97: 			Scope:    scope,
98: 			ResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{
99: 				Group:   r.Group,
100: 				Version: r.Version,
101: 				Kind:    r.Kind,
102: 			},
103: 			Verbs:            r.Verbs,
104: 			ShortNames:       r.ShortNames,
105: 			Categories:       r.Categories,
106: 			SingularResource: r.SingularName,
107: 		}
108: 		apiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)
109: 		parentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	// Loop through all subresources
113: 	for _, r := range list {
114: 		// Split resource name and subresource name
115: 		split := strings.SplitN(r.Name, "/", 2)
116: 
117: 		if len(split) != 2 {
118: 			// Skip parent resources
119: 			continue
120: 		}

122: // Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.
123: type Preconditions struct {
124: 	// Specifies the target UID.
125: 	// +optional
126: 	UID *types.UID `json:"uid,omitempty"`
127: 	// Specifies the target ResourceVersion
128: 	// +optional
129: 	ResourceVersion *string `json:"resourceVersion,omitempty"`
130: }
131: 
132: // NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.
133: func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {
134: 	u := types.UID(uid)
135: 	return &Preconditions{UID: &u}
136: }
137: 
138: func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {
139: 	if p == nil {
140: 		return nil
141: 	}
142: 	objMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return NewInternalError(
145: 			fmt.Errorf("can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w",
146: 				*p,
147: 				obj,
148: 				err))
149: 	}
150: 	if p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {
151: 		err := fmt.Sprintf(
152: 			"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v",
153: 			*p.UID,
154: 			objMeta.GetUID())
155: 		return NewInvalidObjError(key, err)
156: 	}
157: 	if p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {
158: 		err := fmt.Sprintf(
159: 			"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v",
160: 			*p.ResourceVersion,
161: 			objMeta.GetResourceVersion())
162: 		return NewInvalidObjError(key, err)
163: 	}
164: 	return nil
165: }
166: 
167: // Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and
168: // hides all the storage-related operations behind it.
169: type Interface interface {
170: 	// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.
171: 	Versioner() Versioner
172: 
173: 	// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live
174: 	// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be
175: 	// set to the read value from database.
176: 	Create(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error
177: 
178: 	// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.
179: 	// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.
180: 	// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the
181: 	// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.
182: 	// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.
183: 	Delete(
184: 		ctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,
185: 		validateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error
186: 
187: 	// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,
188: 	// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.
189: 	// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,
190: 	// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1
191: 	// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).
192: 	// If resource version is "0", this interface will get current object at given key
193: 	// and send it in an "ADDED" event, before watch starts.
194: 	Watch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)
195: 
196: 	// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either
197: 	// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.
198: 	// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.
199: 	// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will
200: 	// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.
201: 	Get(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error
202: 
203: 	// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object
204: 	// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).
205: 	// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'
206: 	// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.
207: 	// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will
208: 	// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.
209: 	GetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error
210: 
211: 	// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')
212: 	// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.
213: 	// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if
214: 	// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account
215: 	// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.
216: 	// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false
217: 	// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.
218: 	// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized
219: 	// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those
220: 	// contents.
221: 	// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the
222: 	// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.
223: 	// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.
224: 	//
225: 	// Example:
226: 	//
227: 	// s := /* implementation of Interface */
228: 	// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(
229: 	//     "myKey", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,
230: 	//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {
231: 	//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, "input" is reset to
232: 	//       // current contents for "myKey" in database.
233: 	//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.
234: 	//
235: 	//       // Make the modification
236: 	//       curr.Counter++
237: 	//
238: 	//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return
239: 	//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.
240: 	//       return cur, nil, nil
241: 	//    }, cachedExistingObject
242: 	// )
243: 	GuaranteedUpdate(
244: 		ctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,
245: 		preconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error
246: 
247: 	// Stats returns storage stats.
248: 	Stats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)
249: 
250: 	// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.
251: 	ReadinessCheck() error
252: 
253: 	// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the
254: 	// watch response stream as soon as possible.
255: 	// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.
256: 	//
257: 	// If watch is lagging, progress status might:
258: 	// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.
259: 	// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.
260: 	//
261: 	// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.
262: 	// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042
263: 	//
264: 	// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.
265: 	// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.
266: 	RequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error
267: 
268: 	// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.
269: 	// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata
270: 	GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
271: 
272: 	// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.
273: 	EnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error
274: 
275: 	// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.
276: 	// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.
277: 	// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.
278: 	CompactRevision() int64
279: }
280:

57: const (
58: 	// maxLimit is a maximum page limit increase used when fetching objects from etcd.
59: 	// This limit is used only for increasing page size by kube-apiserver. If request
60: 	// specifies larger limit initially, it won't be changed.
61: 	maxLimit = 10000

63: 
64: // authenticatedDataString satisfies the value.Context interface. It uses the key to
65: // authenticate the stored data. This does not defend against reuse of previously
66: // encrypted values under the same key, but will prevent an attacker from using an
67: // encrypted value from a different key. A stronger authenticated data segment would
68: // include the etcd3 Version field (which is incremented on each write to a key and
69: // reset when the key is deleted), but an attacker with write access to etcd can
70: // force deletion and recreation of keys to weaken that angle.
71: type authenticatedDataString string
72: 
73: // AuthenticatedData implements the value.Context interface.
74: func (d authenticatedDataString) AuthenticatedData() []byte {
75: 	return []byte(string(d))
76: }
77: 
78: var _ value.Context = authenticatedDataString("")

80: type store struct {
81: 	client             *kubernetes.Client
82: 	codec              runtime.Codec

89: 	decoder            Decoder
90: 	listErrAggrFactory func() ListErrorAggregator

94: 	compactor      Compactor
95: 
96: 	collectorMux          sync.RWMutex
97: 	resourceSizeEstimator *resourceSizeEstimator
98: }

100: var _ storage.Interface = (*store)(nil)
101: 
102: func (s *store) RequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error {
103: 	// Use watchContext to match ctx metadata provided when creating the watch.
104: 	// In best case scenario we would use the same context that watch was created, but there is no way access it from watchCache.
105: 	return s.client.RequestProgress(s.watchContext(ctx))
106: }

111: 	rev   int64
112: 	data  []byte
113: 	stale bool
114: }

117: // encounters while retrieving object(s) from the storage
118: type ListErrorAggregator interface {
119: 	// Aggregate aggregates the given error from list operation
120: 	// key: it identifies the given object in the storage.

17: // Package value contains methods for assisting with transformation of values in storage.
18: package value
19: 
20: import (
21: 	"bytes"
22: 	"context"
23: 	"fmt"
24: 	"time"
25: 
26: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
27: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors"
28: 	genericapirequest "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
29: 	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
30: )
31: 
32: func init() {
33: 	RegisterMetrics()
34: }
35: 
36: // Context is additional information that a storage transformation may need to verify the data at rest.
37: type Context interface {
38: 	// AuthenticatedData should return an array of bytes that describes the current value. If the value changes,
39: 	// the transformer may report the value as unreadable or tampered. This may be nil if no such description exists
40: 	// or is needed. For additional verification, set this to data that strongly identifies the value, such as
41: 	// the key and creation version of the stored data.
42: 	AuthenticatedData() []byte
43: }
44: 
45: type Read interface {
46: 	// TransformFromStorage may transform the provided data from its underlying storage representation or return an error.
47: 	// Stale is true if the object on disk is stale and a write to etcd should be issued, even if the contents of the object
48: 	// have not changed.
49: 	TransformFromStorage(ctx context.Context, data []byte, dataCtx Context) (out []byte, stale bool, err error)
50: }
51: 
52: type Write interface {
53: 	// TransformToStorage may transform the provided data into the appropriate form in storage or return an error.
54: 	TransformToStorage(ctx context.Context, data []byte, dataCtx Context) (out []byte, err error)
55: }
56: 
57: // Transformer allows a value to be transformed before being read from or written to the underlying store. The methods
58: // must be able to undo the transformation caused by the other.
59: type Transformer interface {
60: 	Read
61: 	Write
62: }
63: 
64: // ResourceTransformers returns a transformer for the provided resource.
65: type ResourceTransformers interface {
66: 	TransformerForResource(resource schema.GroupResource) Transformer
67: }
68: 
69: // DefaultContext is a simple implementation of Context for a slice of bytes.
70: type DefaultContext []byte
71: 
72: // AuthenticatedData returns itself.
73: func (c DefaultContext) AuthenticatedData() []byte { return c }
74: 
75: // PrefixTransformer holds a transformer interface and the prefix that the transformation is located under.
76: type PrefixTransformer struct {
77: 	Prefix      []byte
78: 	Transformer Transformer
79: }
80:

504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)
505: // |           |                                                       |
506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)
507: // |           |                                                       |
508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|
509: // |                                    |
510: // |                                    |
511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)
512: // |                                    |
513: // |                                    |
514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |
516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |
517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |
518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |
519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |
520: // |           |                                       |                                          |
521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |
522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |
523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |
524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |
525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |
526: // |           |                                         |                                        |
527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |
528: // |           |                                         |                                        |
529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|
530: // |                               |                     |
531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())
532: // |                               |
533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh
534: // |                               |
535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)
536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {
537: 	stopCh := ctx.Done()
538: 	delayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration
539: 	shutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated
540: 
541: 	// Clean up resources on shutdown.
542: 	defer s.Destroy()
543: 
544: 	// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket
545: 	if s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {
546: 		go func() {
547: 			defer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)
548: 			klog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))
549: 		}()
550: 	}
551: 
552: 	// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal
553: 	// registration happens during construction of the generic api server
554: 	// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances
555: 	go func() {
556: 		for _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {
557: 			select {
558: 			case <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:
559: 				continue
560: 			case <-stopCh:
561: 				klog.V(1).Infof("haven't completed %s, stop requested", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())
562: 				return
563: 			}
564: 		}
565: 		s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()
566: 		klog.V(1).Infof("%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())
567: 	}()
568: 
569: 	go func() {
570: 		defer delayedStopCh.Signal()
571: 		defer klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", delayedStopCh.Name())
572: 
573: 		<-stopCh
574: 
575: 		// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.
576: 		// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red
577: 		// and stop sending traffic to this server.
578: 		shutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()
579: 		klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())
580: 
581: 		time.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)
582: 	}()
583: 
584: 	// close socket after delayed stopCh
585: 	shutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout
586: 	if s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {
587: 		// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:
588: 		// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight
589: 		//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.
590: 		// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,
591: 		//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so
592: 		//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s
593: 		shutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second
594: 		klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout", "shutdownTimeout", shutdownTimeout)
595: 	}
596: 
597: 	notAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest
598: 	drainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained
599: 	// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.
600: 	// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.
601: 	stopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))
602: 	go func() {
603: 		defer stopHTTPServer(errors.New("time to stop HTTP server"))
604: 
605: 		timeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()
606: 		if s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {
607: 			timeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()
608: 		}
609: 
610: 		<-timeToStopHttpServerCh
611: 	}()
612: 
613: 	// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run
614: 	// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.
615: 	// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.
616: 	if s.AuditBackend != nil {
617: 		if err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {
618: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to run the audit backend: %v", err)
619: 		}
620: 	}
621: 
622: 	stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)
623: 	if err != nil {
624: 		return err
625: 	}
626: 
627: 	httpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening
628: 	go func() {
629: 		<-listenerStoppedCh
630: 		httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()
631: 		klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())
632: 	}()
633: 
634: 	// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has
635: 	// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.
636: 	preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped
637: 	go func() {
638: 		defer klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())
639: 		defer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()
640: 
641: 		// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain
642: 		<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()
643: 
644: 		// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need
645: 		// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing
646: 		// itself from the active servers).
647: 		<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()
648: 	}()
649: 
650: 	// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish
651: 	nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})
652: 	go func() {
653: 		defer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)
654: 		defer klog.V(1).Info("[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained")
655: 
656: 		// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).
657: 		<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()
658: 
659: 		// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.
660: 		// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is
661: 		// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}
662: 		// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe
663: 		// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is
664: 		// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and
665: 		// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming
666: 		// request to get a 'connection refused' error.
667: 		// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming
668: 		// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since
669: 		// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests
670: 		// have been drained.
671: 		// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?
672: 		s.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()
673: 	}()
674: 
675: 	// wait for all in-flight watches to finish
676: 	activeWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})
677: 	go func() {
678: 		defer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)
679: 
680: 		<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()
681: 		if s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {
682: 			klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain")
683: 			return
684: 		}
685: 
686: 		// Wait for all active watches to finish
687: 		grace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod
688: 		activeBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {
689: 			qps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()
690: 			// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to
691: 			//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to
692: 			//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.
693: 			//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change
694: 			//  based on the result from the scale testing.
695: 			if qps < 200 {
696: 				qps = 200
697: 			}
698: 
699: 			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)
700: 			// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed

19: import (

21: 	"fmt"

37: // APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.
38: // This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,
39: // the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,
40: // and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)
41: // FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services
42: type APIServerHandler struct {
43: 	// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter
44: 	// chain and then call the Director.
45: 	FullHandlerChain http.Handler
46: 	// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.
47: 	GoRestfulContainer *restful.Container
48: 	// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.
49: 	// It comes after all filters and the API handling
50: 	// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.
51: 	NonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux
52: 
53: 	// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.
54: 	// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have
55: 	// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)
56: 	// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to
57: 	// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding
58: 	// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints
59: 	// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the
60: 	//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.
61: 	// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to
62: 	// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in
63: 	// order to handle "normal" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think
64: 	// we should consider completely removing gorestful.
65: 	// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.
66: 	Director http.Handler
67: }
68: 
69: // HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.
70: // It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization
71: type HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler
72: 
73: func NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {
74: 	nonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)
75: 	if notFoundHandler != nil {
76: 		nonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	gorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()
80: 	gorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}
81: 	gorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {
82: 		logStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)
83: 	})
84: 	gorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {
85: 		serviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)
86: 	})
87: 
88: 	director := director{
89: 		name:               name,
90: 		goRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,
91: 		nonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	return &APIServerHandler{
95: 		FullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),
96: 		GoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,
97: 		NonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,
98: 		Director:           director,
99: 	}
100: }
101: 
102: // ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /
103: func (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {
104: 	var handledPaths []string
105: 	// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService
106: 	for _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {
107: 		handledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())
108: 	}
109: 	handledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)
110: 	sort.Strings(handledPaths)
111: 
112: 	return handledPaths
113: }
114: 
115: type director struct {
116: 	name               string
117: 	goRestfulContainer *restful.Container
118: 	nonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux
119: }
120: 
121: func (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
122: 	path := req.URL.Path
123: 
124: 	// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path
125: 	for _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {
126: 		switch {
127: 		case ws.RootPath() == "/apis":
128: 			// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.
129: 			// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.
130: 			// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)
131: 			if path == "/apis" || path == "/apis/" {
132: 				klog.V(5).Infof("%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())
133: 				// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices
134: 				// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful
135: 				d.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)
136: 				return
137: 			}
138: 
139: 		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):
140: 			// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match
141: 			if len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {
142: 				klog.V(5).Infof("%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())
143: 				// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices
144: 				// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful
145: 				d.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)
146: 				return
147: 			}
148: 		}
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether
152: 	klog.V(5).Infof("%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful", d.name, req.Method, path)
153: 	d.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)
154: }
155: 
156: // TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?
157: func logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {
158: 	var buffer bytes.Buffer
159: 	buffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("recover from panic situation: - %v\r\n", panicReason))
160: 	for i := 2; ; i++ {
161: 		_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)
162: 		if !ok {
163: 			break
164: 		}
165: 		buffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("    %s:%d\r\n", file, line))
166: 	}
167: 	klog.Errorln(buffer.String())
168: 
169: 	headers := http.Header{}
170: 	if ct := w.Header().Get("Content-Type"); len(ct) > 0 {
171: 		headers.Set("Accept", ct)
172: 	}
173: 	responsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, "", schema.GroupResource{}, "", "", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})
174: }
175: 
176: func serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {
177: 	responsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(
178: 		apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, "", schema.GroupResource{}, "", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),
179: 		s,
180: 		schema.GroupVersion{},
181: 		resp,
182: 		request.Request,
183: 	)
184: }
185: 
186: // ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler
187: func (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
188: 	a.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)
189: }

748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is
749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.
750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.
751: //
752: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid
753: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.
754: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRun(stopCh <-chan struct{}, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {
755: 	ctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)
756: 	return s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx, shutdownTimeout)
757: }
758: 
759: // NonBlockingRunWithContext spawns the secure http server. An error is
760: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.
761: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.
762: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx context.Context, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {
763: 	// Use an internal stop channel to allow cleanup of the listeners on error.
764: 	internalStopCh := make(chan struct{})
765: 	var stoppedCh <-chan struct{}
766: 	var listenerStoppedCh <-chan struct{}
767: 	if s.SecureServingInfo != nil && s.Handler != nil {
768: 		var err error
769: 		stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err = s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, internalStopCh)
770: 		if err != nil {
771: 			close(internalStopCh)
772: 			return nil, nil, err
773: 		}
774: 	}
775: 
776: 	// Now that listener have bound successfully, it is the
777: 	// responsibility of the caller to close the provided channel to
778: 	// ensure cleanup.
779: 	go func() {
780: 		<-ctx.Done()
781: 		close(internalStopCh)
782: 	}()
783: 
784: 	s.RunPostStartHooks(ctx)
785: 
786: 	if _, err := systemd.SdNotify(true, "READY=1\n"); err != nil {
787: 		klog.Errorf("Unable to send systemd daemon successful start message: %v\n", err)
788: 	}
789: 
790: 	return stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, nil
791: }

793: // installAPIResources is a private method for installing the REST storage backing each api groupversionresource
794: func (s *GenericAPIServer) installAPIResources(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, typeConverter managedfields.TypeConverter) error {
795: 	var resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo
796: 	for _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {
797: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {
798: 			klog.Warningf("Skipping API %v because it has no resources.", groupVersion)
799: 			continue
800: 		}
801: 
802: 		apiGroupVersion, err := s.getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion, apiPrefix)
803: 		if err != nil {
804: 			return err
805: 		}
806: 		if apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {
807: 			apiGroupVersion.OptionsExternalVersion = apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion
808: 		}
809: 		apiGroupVersion.TypeConverter = typeConverter
810: 		apiGroupVersion.MaxRequestBodyBytes = s.maxRequestBodyBytes
811: 
812: 		discoveryAPIResources, r, err := apiGroupVersion.InstallREST(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)
813: 
814: 		if err != nil {
815: 			return fmt.Errorf("unable to setup API %v: %v", apiGroupInfo, err)
816: 		}
817: 		resourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, r...)
818: 
819: 		// Aggregated discovery only aggregates resources under /apis
820: 		if apiPrefix == APIGroupPrefix {
821: 			s.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(
822: 				groupVersion.Group,
823: 				apidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{
824: 					Freshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,
825: 					Version:   groupVersion.Version,
826: 					Resources: discoveryAPIResources,
827: 				},
828: 			)
829: 		} else {
830: 			// There is only one group version for legacy resources, priority can be defaulted to 0.
831: 			s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(
832: 				groupVersion.Group,
833: 				apidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{
834: 					Freshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,
835: 					Version:   groupVersion.Version,
836: 					Resources: discoveryAPIResources,
837: 				},
838: 			)
839: 		}
840: 
841: 	}
842: 
843: 	s.RegisterDestroyFunc(apiGroupInfo.destroyStorage)
844: 
845: 	if s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionAPI) &&
846: 		s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.APIServerIdentity) {
847: 		// API installation happens before we start listening on the handlers,
848: 		// therefore it is safe to register ResourceInfos here. The handler will block
849: 		// write requests until the storage versions of the targeting resources are updated.
850: 		s.StorageVersionManager.AddResourceInfo(resourceInfos...)
851: 	}
852: 
853: 	return nil
854: }
855: 
856: // InstallLegacyAPIGroup exposes the given legacy api group in the API.
857: // The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the
858: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.
859: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallLegacyAPIGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {
860: 	if !s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.Has(apiPrefix) {
861: 		return fmt.Errorf("%q is not in the allowed legacy API prefixes: %v", apiPrefix, s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.List())
862: 	}
863: 
864: 	openAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo)
865: 	if err != nil {
866: 		return fmt.Errorf("unable to get openapi models: %v", err)
867: 	}
868: 
869: 	if err := s.installAPIResources(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {
870: 		return err
871: 	}
872: 
873: 	// Install the version handler.
874: 	// Add a handler at /<apiPrefix> to enumerate the supported api versions.
875: 	legacyRootAPIHandler := discovery.NewLegacyRootAPIHandler(s.discoveryAddresses, s.Serializer, apiPrefix)
876: 	// No peer-to-peer discovery for legacy API group.
877: 	wrapped := discoveryendpoint.WrapAggregatedDiscoveryToHandler(legacyRootAPIHandler, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager)
878: 	s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(wrapped.GenerateWebService("/api", metav1.APIVersions{}))
879: 	s.registerStorageReadinessCheck("", apiGroupInfo)
880: 
881: 	return nil
882: }
883: 
884: // InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.
885: // The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the
886: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.
887: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {
888: 	for _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {
889: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {
890: 			return fmt.Errorf("no version priority set for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
891: 		}
892: 		// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.
893: 		// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin
894: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {
895: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
896: 		}
897: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {
898: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
899: 		}
900: 	}

17: package authenticator
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"net/http"
22: 
23: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
24: )
25: 
26: // Token checks a string value against a backing authentication store and
27: // returns a Response or an error if the token could not be checked.
28: type Token interface {
29: 	AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)
30: }
31: 
32: // Request attempts to extract authentication information from a request and
33: // returns a Response or an error if the request could not be checked.
34: type Request interface {
35: 	AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)
36: }
37: 
38: // TokenFunc is a function that implements the Token interface.
39: type TokenFunc func(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)
40: 
41: // AuthenticateToken implements authenticator.Token.
42: func (f TokenFunc) AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error) {
43: 	return f(ctx, token)
44: }
45: 
46: // RequestFunc is a function that implements the Request interface.
47: type RequestFunc func(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)
48: 
49: // AuthenticateRequest implements authenticator.Request.
50: func (f RequestFunc) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error) {
51: 	return f(req)
52: }
53: 
54: // Response is the struct returned by authenticator interfaces upon successful
55: // authentication. It contains information about whether the authenticator
56: // authenticated the request, information about the context of the
57: // authentication, and information about the authenticated user.
58: type Response struct {
59: 	// Audiences is the set of audiences the authenticator was able to validate
60: 	// the token against. If the authenticator is not audience aware, this field
61: 	// will be empty.
62: 	Audiences Audiences
63: 	// User is the UserInfo associated with the authentication context.
64: 	User user.Info
65: }

29: // Attributes is an interface used by an Authorizer to get information about a request
30: // that is used to make an authorization decision.
31: type Attributes interface {
32: 	// GetUser returns the user.Info object to authorize
33: 	GetUser() user.Info
34: 
35: 	// GetVerb returns the kube verb associated with API requests (this includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, deletecollection, and proxy),
36: 	// or the lowercased HTTP verb associated with non-API requests (this includes get, put, post, patch, and delete)
37: 	GetVerb() string
38: 
39: 	// When IsReadOnly() == true, the request has no side effects, other than
40: 	// caching, logging, and other incidentals.
41: 	IsReadOnly() bool
42: 
43: 	// The namespace of the object, if a request is for a REST object.
44: 	GetNamespace() string
45: 
46: 	// The kind of object, if a request is for a REST object.
47: 	GetResource() string
48: 
49: 	// GetSubresource returns the subresource being requested, if present
50: 	GetSubresource() string
51: 
52: 	// GetName returns the name of the object as parsed off the request.  This will not be present for all request types, but
53: 	// will be present for: get, update, delete
54: 	GetName() string
55: 
56: 	// The group of the resource, if a request is for a REST object.
57: 	GetAPIGroup() string
58: 
59: 	// GetAPIVersion returns the version of the group requested, if a request is for a REST object.
60: 	GetAPIVersion() string
61: 
62: 	// IsResourceRequest returns true for requests to API resources, like /api/v1/nodes,
63: 	// and false for non-resource endpoints like /api, /healthz
64: 	IsResourceRequest() bool
65: 
66: 	// GetPath returns the path of the request
67: 	GetPath() string
68: 
69: 	// ParseFieldSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.
70: 	// It returns an error if the field selector cannot be parsed.
71: 	// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.
72: 	GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)
73: 
74: 	// ParseLabelSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.
75: 	// It returns an error if the label selector cannot be parsed.
76: 	// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.
77: 	GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)
78: }
79: 
80: // Authorizer makes an authorization decision based on information gained by making
81: // zero or more calls to methods of the Attributes interface.  It returns nil when an action is
82: // authorized, otherwise it returns an error.
83: type Authorizer interface {
84: 	Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)
85: }
86: 
87: type AuthorizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)
88: 
89: func (f AuthorizerFunc) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error) {
90: 	return f(ctx, a)
91: }
92: 
93: // RuleResolver provides a mechanism for resolving the list of rules that apply to a given user within a namespace.
94: type RuleResolver interface {
95: 	// RulesFor get the list of cluster wide rules, the list of rules in the specific namespace, incomplete status and errors.
96: 	RulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)
97: }
98:

100: type RequestAttributesGetter interface {

17: package options
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"github.com/spf13/pflag"
21: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
22: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission"
23: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server"
24: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend"
25: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature"
26: 	"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
27: 	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
28: 	"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate"
29: )
30: 
31: // RecommendedOptions contains the recommended options for running an API server.
32: // If you add something to this list, it should be in a logical grouping.
33: // Each of them can be nil to leave the feature unconfigured on ApplyTo.
34: type RecommendedOptions struct {
35: 	Etcd           *EtcdOptions
36: 	SecureServing  *SecureServingOptionsWithLoopback
37: 	Authentication *DelegatingAuthenticationOptions
38: 	Authorization  *DelegatingAuthorizationOptions
39: 	Audit          *AuditOptions
40: 	Features       *FeatureOptions
41: 	CoreAPI        *CoreAPIOptions
42: 
43: 	// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.
44: 	FeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate
45: 	// ExtraAdmissionInitializers is called once after all ApplyTo from the options above, to pass the returned
46: 	// admission plugin initializers to Admission.ApplyTo.
47: 	ExtraAdmissionInitializers func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error)
48: 	Admission                  *AdmissionOptions
49: 	// API Server Egress Selector is used to control outbound traffic from the API Server
50: 	EgressSelector *EgressSelectorOptions
51: 	// Traces contains options to control distributed request tracing.
52: 	Traces *TracingOptions
53: }
54: 
55: func NewRecommendedOptions(prefix string, codec runtime.Codec) *RecommendedOptions {
56: 	sso := NewSecureServingOptions()
57: 
58: 	// We are composing recommended options for an aggregated api-server,
59: 	// whose client is typically a proxy multiplexing many operations ---
60: 	// notably including long-running ones --- into one HTTP/2 connection
61: 	// into this server.  So allow many concurrent operations.
62: 	sso.HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection = 1000
63: 
64: 	return &RecommendedOptions{
65: 		Etcd:           NewEtcdOptions(storagebackend.NewDefaultConfig(prefix, codec)),
66: 		SecureServing:  sso.WithLoopback(),
67: 		Authentication: NewDelegatingAuthenticationOptions(),
68: 		Authorization:  NewDelegatingAuthorizationOptions(),
69: 		Audit:          NewAuditOptions(),
70: 		Features:       NewFeatureOptions(),
71: 		CoreAPI:        NewCoreAPIOptions(),
72: 		// Wired a global by default that sadly people will abuse to have different meanings in different repos.
73: 		// Please consider creating your own FeatureGate so you can have a consistent meaning for what a variable contains
74: 		// across different repos.  Future you will thank you.
75: 		FeatureGate:                feature.DefaultFeatureGate,
76: 		ExtraAdmissionInitializers: func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error) { return nil, nil },
77: 		Admission:                  NewAdmissionOptions(),
78: 		EgressSelector:             NewEgressSelectorOptions(),
79: 		Traces:                     NewTracingOptions(),
80: 	}
81: }
82: 
83: func (o *RecommendedOptions) AddFlags(fs *pflag.FlagSet) {
84: 	o.Etcd.AddFlags(fs)
85: 	o.SecureServing.AddFlags(fs)
86: 	o.Authentication.AddFlags(fs)
87: 	o.Authorization.AddFlags(fs)
88: 	o.Audit.AddFlags(fs)
89: 	o.Features.AddFlags(fs)
90: 	o.CoreAPI.AddFlags(fs)
91: 	o.Admission.AddFlags(fs)
92: 	o.EgressSelector.AddFlags(fs)
93: 	o.Traces.AddFlags(fs)
94: }
95: 
96: // ApplyTo adds RecommendedOptions to the server configuration.
97: // pluginInitializers can be empty, it is only need for additional initializers.
98: func (o *RecommendedOptions) ApplyTo(config *server.RecommendedConfig) error {
99: 	if err := o.Etcd.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
100: 		return err
101: 	}
102: 	if err := o.EgressSelector.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
103: 		return err
104: 	}
105: 	if err := o.Traces.ApplyTo(config.Config.EgressSelector, &config.Config); err != nil {
106: 		return err
107: 	}
108: 	if err := o.SecureServing.ApplyToConfig(&config.Config); err != nil {
109: 		return err
110: 	}
111: 	if err := o.Authentication.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authentication, config.SecureServing, config.OpenAPIConfig); err != nil {
112: 		return err
113: 	}
114: 	if err := o.Authorization.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authorization); err != nil {
115: 		return err
116: 	}
117: 	if err := o.Audit.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {
118: 		return err
119: 	}
120: 	if err := o.CoreAPI.ApplyTo(config); err != nil {
121: 		return err
122: 	}
123: 	var kubeClient kubernetes.Interface
124: 	var dynamicClient dynamic.Interface
125: 	if config.ClientConfig != nil {
126: 		var err error
127: 		kubeClient, err = kubernetes.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)
128: 		if err != nil {
129: 			return err
130: 		}
131: 		dynamicClient, err = dynamic.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)
132: 		if err != nil {
133: 			return err
134: 		}
135: 	}
136: 	if err := o.Features.ApplyTo(&config.Config, kubeClient, config.SharedInformerFactory); err != nil {
137: 		return err
138: 	}
139: 	initializers, err := o.ExtraAdmissionInitializers(config)
140: 	if err != nil {
141: 		return err
142: 	}
143: 	if err := o.Admission.ApplyTo(&config.Config, config.SharedInformerFactory, kubeClient, dynamicClient, o.FeatureGate,
144: 		config.EffectiveVersion,
145: 		initializers...); err != nil {
146: 		return err
147: 	}
148: 	return nil
149: }

153: 	errors = append(errors, o.Etcd.Validate()...)
154: 	errors = append(errors, o.SecureServing.Validate()...)
155: 	errors = append(errors, o.Authentication.Validate()...)
